name: Vikram Rao
tagline: Just tell me if it works and how much it costs — skip the story

demographics.occupation: Engineering Manager at an automotive parts company

aspiration_drivers: getting the job done right the first time
providing well for his family without waste

backstory: Vikram is an engineer to his core. He approaches every purchase like a design problem — define requirements, evaluate options, pick the one with the best specs-to-cost ratio. He does not get excited by brands or trends. He gets excited by things that work well and last long. His wife sometimes wishes he would just pick the nice-looking option instead of spending 20 minutes comparing spec sheets. He bought the same model of running shoes three times because they fit well and did not fall apart.
current_life_context: His toddler has sensitive skin and his wife asked him to research baby-safe skincare. He is approaching this with the same rigor he applies to buying power tools — reading ingredient lists, checking certifications, ignoring anything that says 'natural' without data to back it up.
inner_monologue_style: Structured and no-nonsense. Thinks in bullet points: 'Ok — what problem does this solve? What are the specs? What does it cost per unit? Is there a better option at this price? Done.' Rarely uses emotional language. Will occasionally be pleasantly surprised by good design but will not admit it influenced his decision.
